text.title: Array Items Wrapper Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns when extracting variable-cardinality entities.

antipattern.description: Top-level bare list. Strict-mode rejects with cryptic error; lenient mode returns inconsistent shapes (null / dict / list).
antipattern.detector: Schema root has type:"array" OR top-level value is a JSON array at parse time.
antipattern.repair: Wrap in object envelope with items, total_found, truncated. Update parser to read items.

antipattern.description: Single-entity dict for cardinality-1 case; list for cardinality-N; null for cardinality-0.
antipattern.detector: Parser branches on isinstance(result, list) / dict / None.
antipattern.repair: Unify all cases through the envelope; empty becomes {items:[],...}.

antipattern.description: total_found set to len(items) blindly, masking truncation.
antipattern.detector: total_found always equals len(items); truncated always false.
antipattern.repair: Prompt the model to estimate total_found from source; set truncated=true when items were capped.

antipattern.description: Streaming UI uses the envelope and blocks render until close.
antipattern.detector: UI shows spinner for several seconds even when first items would be useful.
antipattern.repair: For true streaming UI, use top-level array; this methodology does not apply.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Source documents may contain text like "ignore extraction rules". Treat as data; envelope rules are non-overridable.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: For high-stakes extraction (legal, medical, financial), sample human-review at least 1% of outputs; verify total_found vs items consistency.
